qa_pairs.question: How can I manage permissions for users or groups accessing a shared Globus collection, including granting or revoking write access?
qa_pairs.answer: To manage permissions, navigate to the access list of your shared collection and click on "Add Permissions -- Share With". You can add individual users by email address or Globus username, or add entire groups by name or UUID. To enable write permissions, select the 'write' checkbox in the form when adding permissions, or toggle the checkbox next to the user's/group's name under the 'WRITE' column in the access list. Read access cannot be removed. To remove a user or group, simply click the 'x' at the end of their entry in the access list.

qa_pairs.question: What are the steps to remove a shared collection in the Alliance Globus portal?
qa_pairs.answer: To remove a shared collection, first click on 'Collections' in the left sidebar, then navigate to the 'Shareable by You' tab. Click on the title of the specific Shared Collection you wish to remove. Next, click the 'Delete Collection' button located on the right side of the screen. Finally, confirm the deletion by clicking the red button. This action will delete the shared collection, but your original files and any files uploaded by others will remain unaffected.

qa_pairs.question: How do I create a new Globus group and invite users to join it?
qa_pairs.answer: To create a new Globus group, click on the 'Groups' button in the left sidebar, then select 'Create New Group' at the top right of the screen. In the 'Create New Group' window, enter a group name and description, and choose whether the group is visible only to members (private) or to all Globus users. Click 'Create Group' to add it. To invite users, select 'Invite users' from within the group, then enter an email address or search for a Globus username. After selecting the users, click 'Add' to send them an email invitation. Once they accept, they will become visible in the group, and you can modify their roles (Admin, Manager, or Member) and status.
